title: hayata tabiyahanensis var. merrillii
intro: Hayata tabiyahanensis var. merrillii, also known as the Merrill's variety of Hayata tabiyahanensis, is a plant species classified under the family Annonaceae. It is a type of tree native to the Philippines, and its bark and leaves are traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of Hayata tabiyahanensis var. merrillii are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
